commit.message:
refactor!: rename hof.TapErr to hof.OnErr — handler registration semantics

result.IfErr which conditionally checks an existing value. Also adds
cmd.complete to mk script.

commit.message:
feat: L3 deep repair recovery tier (v1.4.0)

Complete L3 recovery with 5 automated repair strategies:
- Network repair: DNS flush, TUN interface reset, route detection
- Process resurrection: reinstall gateway binary if missing
- Dependency health: detect corrupted node_modules, rebuild
- Safe mode boot: start with minimal config when normal restart fails
- Disk cleanup: truncate logs, delete rotated files, clear temp dirs

Recovery cascade is now L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 (was L1 → L2 → L4).
New config: l3MaxAttempts, l3CooldownMs, l3SafeModeArgs.
All docs, roadmap, changelog, and architecture updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate CLS 1.0 caused by fixed-position LoadingPage during streaming

Root cause: loading.tsx renders a position:fixed overlay (zero flow height).
During Next.js streaming, the layout shell sends Header + empty <main> + Footer.
With no flow content in <main>, the footer sits in the viewport. When page
content streams in, the footer shifts ~5000px down — CLS = 1.000.

Fix: Add an invisible min-h-screen flow spacer inside LoadingPage so the footer
is pushed below the viewport from the first frame. Also add min-h-screen to
<main> as a belt-and-suspenders safeguard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lock): use separate mise.<env>.lock files instead of env tags (#8523)

## Summary
- Environment-specific configs now get their own lockfiles
(`mise.test.toml` → `mise.test.lock`) instead of using `env = ["test"]`
tags in a single `mise.lock`
- Removes `env` field from `LockfileTool` struct and all env-tag
merge/resolution logic
- Fixes macOS-incompatible `sed` in `test_lockfile_provenance` (replaced
with portable `awk`)

### Motivation (discussed in #8000)
- **CI cache invalidation**: Dev tool version bumps no longer touch
`mise.lock`, so CI caches aren't busted unnecessarily
- **Checksum consistency**: No risk of divergent checksums for the same
tool across env tags in a single file
- **Simplicity**: ~100 fewer lines of code with no env-tag tracking,
merging, or priority resolution

### Lockfile mapping
| Config file | Lockfile |
|---|---|
| `mise.toml` | `mise.lock` |
| `mise.test.toml` | `mise.test.lock` |
| `mise.local.toml` | `mise.local.lock` |
| `mise.test.local.toml` | `mise.test.local.lock` |

### Backwards compatibility
Old lockfiles with `env = [...]` fields are read without error — the
field is silently discarded. Running `mise lock` migrates to the new
format automatically.

Closes #8000

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test` — 528 unit tests pass
- [x] `test_lock_env` — env lockfile creation
- [x] `test_lock_env_ci` — CI isolation (no MISE_ENV = only mise.lock)
- [x] `test_lockfile_env` — `mise use` with MISE_ENV
- [x] `test_lockfile_env_resolution` — version resolution across
lockfiles
- [x] `test_lockfile_env_base_override` — tool in both base and env
configs
- [x] `test_lockfile_provenance` — provenance test (fixed sed → awk)
- [x] `test_lockfile_local`, `test_lockfile_use`,
`test_lockfile_install` — no regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core lockfile pathing, generation, and version resolution
behavior, which can impact reproducibility and tool selection across
environments if edge cases are missed. Changes are well-covered by
updated/new e2e tests, reducing regression risk.
> 
> **Overview**
> Switches environment-specific tool locking from `env = [...]` tags
inside a single `mise.lock` to **per-environment lockfiles**
(`mise.<env>.lock` / `mise.<env>.local.lock`), and updates `mise lock`
to generate/prune/update each lockfile target based on its originating
config file(s).
> 
> Removes the `env` field from `LockfileTool` and deletes all
env-merge/priority-resolution logic, while keeping backwards
compatibility by silently ignoring legacy `env` fields when reading old
lockfiles; lockfile loading now prefers env-specific lockfiles (when
`MISE_ENV` is set) ahead of base/local lockfiles.
> 
> Updates docs and e2e coverage for the new lockfile mapping (including
CI isolation), and makes the provenance test portable by replacing
macOS-incompatible `sed` editing with `awk`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
090bfd9ecea129c21c40f3190320407533f50ffd. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

agent's editable step kinds so the Android app can list, improve, and
publish content for these steps. The instruction field is used as the
primary content field for pedagogical steps instead of message.

Standardize UI consistency across all pages

Shared utilities:
- Extract formatTokenCount, formatRunTime, formatJobDate, STATUS_CONFIG
  to lib/format.ts — single source of truth used by all consumers
- Create ConfirmDialog component replacing browser confirm() dialogs

Status badges:
- Unified badge styling across all 4 job listing surfaces
- TeamJobList now matches dashboard pattern (text-[10px], size-2.5 icons,
  ring-1 for active jobs instead of animate-pulse-subtle)
- Execution trace shows status labels instead of raw strings

Jobs listing (/jobs):
- Now shows title (with prompt as subtitle) matching dashboard/team views
- Added confidence, run time, and updatedAt to query and cards
- Uses shared formatJobDate instead of inline toLocaleDateString

Page headers:
- All pages now use gradient text (from-purple-700 to-blue-600)
- Fixed: Agent Catalog, Create Team, Team Settings, Solution Settings,
  team/solution New Job forms

Back navigation:
- Standardized to ghost button pattern across all pages
- Fixed: teams/new, skills/[id], skills/new

Confirmation dialogs:
- Replaced browser confirm() with styled ConfirmDialog on publish
  solution, archive solution, and delete team actions
- Consistent with lightbox/modal design language (backdrop, escape key,
  body scroll lock)

Labeling:
- Deliverables lightbox header: "Output" → "Results" (matches button)
- Job detail date: raw toLocaleString → formatJobDate

- Revertir main.ts de ambas apps a bootstrapApplication estándar
- Actualizar app.routes.ts de landing-spa: /demo usa loadChildren local (demo.routes.ts)

dependencias adicionales.

commit.message:
feat: OpenAPI tool adapter for auto-generating AgentTools from specs (#11)

* feat: add OpenAPI tool adapter for auto-generating AgentTools from specs

Parse OpenAPI 3.0 specs (JSON/YAML) and create one AgentTool per operation,
with full $ref resolution, auth support, and operation filtering. Feature-gated
behind `openapi` Cargo feature to keep base crate lean.

Closes #7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings for OpenAPI adapter

- Return errors as ToolResult content instead of Err(ToolError) so LLM
  can self-correct (CLAUDE.md convention)
- Apply config timeout to from_url() spec fetch instead of using default
  reqwest client
- Only set has_body=true when application/json content is found
- Omit "required" from JSON Schema when array is empty (spec compliance)
- Fall back to "_request_body" key when a param named "body" exists
- Return Ok(vec![]) instead of Err(NoOperations) for MCP consistency
- Remove dead YamlError and NoOperations error variants
- Fix misleading with_tools() comment in mod.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update mdbook with openapi feature, complete API reference, and fix config examples

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix-tab-flash-set-tab-before-auth-gate

Move setTab() to before renderAuthGate() in init(). Previously the
app shell was made visible (by renderAuthGate) while the default
Composer is-active class was still set from the static HTML, causing
a brief Composer flash before setTab() corrected it. Now the correct
tab is already active in the DOM before app-shell is ever shown.

commit.message:
Add MCP server binary with HTTP/SSE transport and desktop overlay tools

Expose ~73 desktop automation tools to Claude Code via a standalone MCP server
binary (mcp_desktop_tools). Supports two transport modes:
- stdio (default): standard JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout
- HTTP/SSE (--http [port]): avoids Windows stdio pipe stalling issues

Key additions:
- MCP server binary at src/mcp_server.rs with 30s tool timeouts and JPEG
  image compression (PNG→1280px→JPEG q75, ~80% size reduction)
- Status overlay tools (show/update/hide_status_overlay) for on-screen
  progress bar during desktop automation via PowerShell WinForms
- Desktop notification tool (send_notification) for toast alerts
- dispatch_desktop_tool() router and DESKTOP_TOOL_NAMES registry
- StreamableHttpService integration with axum for HTTP/SSE mode

A pill indicator shows "Upgrading to high quality…" while the Pro
render is in flight.
